visa_types.overview:
Overview
- Foreign  workers who wish to work in Belgium as employees (i.e. working under an  employment contract) must hold a work permit, and the visa is needed  accordingly.
- Foreign  nationals wishing to settle in Belgium and be self-employed must hold a  professional card authorising them to exercise their profession, and the visa  is needed accordingly.
- For more information, please  visit the website of the Immigration Office in Brussels:
https://dofi.ibz.be/en (https://dofi.ibz.be/en)
- Applicants applying for Belgium Long Stay Visa (D Visa) Category are requested to appear in person at the visa application center to register their biometrics which includes scans of all fingerprints and live digital photo.
For more information, please visit the website of the Embassy and Consulates of Belgium in China: https://china.diplomatie.belgium.be/en (https://china.diplomatie.belgium.be/en)
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
- Foreign  workers who wish to work in Belgium as employees (i.e. working under an  employment contract) must hold a work permit, and the visa is needed  accordingly.
- Foreign  nationals wishing to settle in Belgium and be self-employed must hold a  professional card authorising them to exercise their profession, and the visa  is needed accordingly.
- For more information, please  visit the website of the Immigration Office in Brussels:
https://dofi.ibz.be/en (https://dofi.ibz.be/en)
- Applicants applying for Belgium Long Stay Visa (D Visa) Category are requested to appear in person at the visa application center to register their biometrics which includes scans of all fingerprints and live digital photo.
For more information, please visit the website of the Embassy and Consulates of Belgium in China: https://china.diplomatie.belgium.be/en (https://china.diplomatie.belgium.be/en)

visa_types.processing_time:
Applications received at the Belgian Embassy/Consulate General will be processed within 2 and 15 working days, provided that a complete file is submitted. We recommend that you submit your applications 15 days prior to the intended departure date.
Applications can be submitted up to 180 days in advance.
In case the file has to be submitted for decision to the Immigration Office in Brussels, the processing time may take more than 15 days.
Passports are kept at the Embassy for the duration of the processing of the visa application. Should you have an urgent need for your passport, please contact the Visa Application Centre where your application has been submitted or the Belgian Embassy/Consulate General via email to request a temporary withdrawal of your passport.
For more information on the processing time, please visit the website of the Belgian Ministry of Foreign Affairs (https://dofi.ibz.be/en).
visa_types.overview:
Overview
If the purpose of is to participate the professional training in  Belgium, you need to apply for Professional Training Schengen visa. This  service is available for residents in China who wish to apply for a Belgian  Visa. 
As of 13 July 2020, all visa applications for Belgium introduced in China, will be processed by the Embassy of Belgium in Beijing.
Exceptions will be the visa applications of Chinese citizens travelling with official and public affairs passports, family members of EU citizens, and “Fast Track” business applications. These categories will still be forwarded to the Consulate-General in Shanghai and Guangzhou for processing based on the address stated on the Hukou or Temporary resident card.
- The jurisdiction of the Consulate General of Belgium in Guangzhou includes the following provinces: Guangdong, Yunnan, Autonomous Region of Guangxi, Fujian, and Hainan,
- The jurisdiction of the Consulate General of Belgium in Shanghai includes the following provinces: Jiangsu, Anhui, Zhejiang and Shanghai.
- All other Chinese provinces and Autonomous Regions belong to the jurisdiction of the Embassy of Belgium in Beijing.
Applicants may submit applications in one of the 15 Visa Application Centres of Belgium around China.
For more information, please visit the website of the Embassy/Consulates of Belgium in China: https://china.diplomatie.belgium.be/en (https://china.diplomatie.belgium.be/en)
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If the purpose of is to participate the professional training in  Belgium, you need to apply for Professional Training Schengen visa. This  service is available for residents in China who wish to apply for a Belgian  Visa. 
As of 13 July 2020, all visa applications for Belgium introduced in China, will be processed by the Embassy of Belgium in Beijing.
Exceptions will be the visa applications of Chinese citizens travelling with official and public affairs passports, family members of EU citizens, and “Fast Track” business applications. These categories will still be forwarded to the Consulate-General in Shanghai and Guangzhou for processing based on the address stated on the Hukou or Temporary resident card.
- The jurisdiction of the Consulate General of Belgium in Guangzhou includes the following provinces: Guangdong, Yunnan, Autonomous Region of Guangxi, Fujian, and Hainan,
- The jurisdiction of the Consulate General of Belgium in Shanghai includes the following provinces: Jiangsu, Anhui, Zhejiang and Shanghai.
- All other Chinese provinces and Autonomous Regions belong to the jurisdiction of the Embassy of Belgium in Beijing.
Applicants may submit applications in one of the 15 Visa Application Centres of Belgium around China.
For more information, please visit the website of the Embassy/Consulates of Belgium in China: https://china.diplomatie.belgium.be/en (https://china.diplomatie.belgium.be/en)

## Visa Fees

[Table:   Business / Conference / Fair]
Visa Service Fee: 185 RMB per applicant.
Added Value Service fees: for more information, please check the Premium services (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/chn/en/bel/premium-services) page.

## Documents Required
To apply for a short term Schengen visa you will need multiple  documents. Below are the requirements for Professional  Training. 
Check list for Professional Training 
Specific insurance requirements 

## Photo Specifications
Please click here to get the new photograph requirements.

## Processing Time
Applications received at the Belgian Embassy/Consulate General will be processed within 2 and 15 working days, provided that a complete file is submitted. We recommend that you submit your applications 15 days prior to the intended departure date.
Applications can be submitted up to 180 days in advance.
In case the file has to be submitted for decision to the Immigration Office in Brussels, the processing time may take more than 15 days.
Passports are kept at the Embassy for the duration of the processing of the visa application. Should you have an urgent need for your passport, please contact the Visa Application Centre where your application has been submitted or the Belgian Embassy/Consulate General via email to request a temporary withdrawal of your passport.
For more information on the processing time, please visit the website of the Belgian Ministry of Foreign Affairs (https://dofi.ibz.be/en).

Overview
If you are no longer in possession of your A-, B-, C-, D-, H-, K-, L- or M- residence permit, whether it is still valid or expired, you do not automatically have the right to return to Belgium. You should apply for the authorization to return by submitting an application for a Return D Visa.
As of 13 July 2020, all visa applications for Belgium introduced in China, will be processed by the Embassy of Belgium in Beijing.
Exceptions will be the visa applications of Chinese citizens travelling with official and public affairs passports, family members of EU citizens, and “Fast Track” business applications. These categories will still be forwarded to the Consulate-General in Shanghai and Guangzhou for processing based on the address stated on the Hukou or Temporary resident card.
- The jurisdiction of the Consulate General of Belgium in Guangzhou includes the following provinces: Guangdong, Yunnan, Autonomous Region of Guangxi, Fujian, and Hainan,
- The jurisdiction of the Consulate General of Belgium in Shanghai includes the following provinces: Jiangsu, Anhui, Zhejiang and Shanghai.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
If the purpose of traveling is Business meetings, Internal trainings, Conference or Attending any trade fair. You need to apply for a Business / Conference / Fair short term Schengen visa. This service is available for residents in China who wish to apply for a Belgian Visa.
As of 13 July 2020, all visa applications for Belgium introduced in China, will be processed by the Embassy of Belgium in Beijing.
Exceptions will be the visa applications of Chinese citizens travelling with official and public affairs passports, family members of EU citizens, and “Fast Track” business applications. These categories will still be forwarded to the Consulate-General in Shanghai and Guangzhou for processing based on the address stated on the Hukou or Temporary resident card.
- The jurisdiction of the Consulate General of Belgium in Guangzhou includes the following provinces: Guangdong, Yunnan, Autonomous Region of Guangxi, Fujian, and Hainan,
- The jurisdiction of the Consulate General of Belgium in Shanghai includes the following provinces: Jiangsu, Anhui, Zhejiang and Shanghai.
- All other Chinese provinces and Autonomous Regions belong to the jurisdiction of the Embassy of Belgium in Beijing.
Applicants may submit applications in one of the 15 Visa Application Centres of Belgium around China.
For more information, please visit the website of the Embassy/Consulates of Belgium in China: https://china.diplomatie.belgium.be/en (https://china.diplomatie.belgium.be/en)

visa_types.name: Short Stay
visa_types.overview:
Short-Stay visa (C type) entitles its holder to travel throughout the Schengen States for up to 90 days in any 180-day period. Visas for visits exceeding that period remain subject to national procedures.Click here (https://eeas.europa.eu/delegations/china/15408/node/15408_en) to know more about Schengen Visa. Guarantees for return (assessment of migration risk): The applicant is requested to provide as much information as possible to document his/her  family background, professional and socio-economic status, ownership of land or real estate (for instance: proof of family bonds with his/her  country of origin, proof of regular income and stable source of revenues from the applicant and/or his/her partner, proof of property, etc.) 
This information is essential to correctly judge the intention of the applicant to leave the Schengen area before the expiry of his/her visa.
In  case you have already submitted your fingerprints for a previous Schengen visa  application in the last 59 months, you are not required to submit them again.
However, if you are uncertain whether your fingerprints have been captured for a  Schengen visa within the last 59 months, you are advised to appear in person.
Kindly please note that, if it would appear that the fingerprints prove not to be  present in the VIS system, you will be asked to reappear in person for new  biometric data collection. To avoid this possible complication, you have the  right to ask for your fingerprints to be taken again.
- Business / Conference / Fair (#business/conference/fair)
- Tourism (#tourism)
- Family and Friends Visit (#familyandfriendsvisit)
- Cultural and Sports Events (#culturalandsportsevents)
- ADS (#ads)
- Professional Training (#professionaltraining)
- Return C (Right to return to Belgium for F or F+ Card) (#returnc(righttoreturntobelgiumforforf+card))

- The jurisdiction of the Consulate General of Belgium in Guangzhou includes the following provinces: Guangdong, Yunnan, Autonomous Region of Guangxi, Fujian, and Hainan,
- The jurisdiction of the Consulate General of Belgium in Shanghai includes the following provinces: Jiangsu, Anhui, Zhejiang and Shanghai.
- All other Chinese provinces and Autonomous Regions belong to the jurisdiction of the Embassy of Belgium in Beijing.
Applicants may submit applications in one of the 15 Visa Application Centres of Belgium around China.
For more information, please visit the website of the Embassy/Consulates of Belgium in China: https://china.diplomatie.belgium.be/en (https://china.diplomatie.belgium.be/en)

## Overview
If the purpose of traveling is to get married in Belgium you need to apply for Marry in Belgium Schengen visa. This service is available for residents in China who wish to apply for a Belgian Visa. 
As of 13 July 2020, all visa applications for Belgium introduced in China, will be processed by the Embassy of Belgium in Beijing.
Exceptions will be the visa applications of Chinese citizens travelling with official and public affairs passports, family members of EU citizens, and “Fast Track” business applications. These categories will still be forwarded to the Consulate-General in Shanghai and Guangzhou for processing based on the address stated on the Hukou or Temporary resident card.
- The jurisdiction of the Consulate General of Belgium in Guangzhou includes the following provinces: Guangdong, Yunnan, Autonomous Region of Guangxi, Fujian, and Hainan,
- The jurisdiction of the Consulate General of Belgium in Shanghai includes the following provinces: Jiangsu, Anhui, Zhejiang and Shanghai.
- All other Chinese provinces and Autonomous Regions belong to the jurisdiction of the Embassy of Belgium in Beijing.
Applicants may submit applications in one of the 15 Visa Application Centres of Belgium around China.
For more information, please visit the website of the Embassy/Consulates of Belgium in China: https://china.diplomatie.belgium.be/en (https://china.diplomatie.belgium.be/en)

## Visa Fees

[Table:   Marriage in Belgium]
Visa Service Fee: 185 RMB per applicant.
Added Value Service fees: for more information, please check the Premium services (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/chn/en/bel/premium-services) page.
Administrative fee for LONG STAY visas for Belgium
Any person who is not exempt and who wishes to stay in Belgium for more than 90 days must pay a fee. The fee covers the costs of processing the residence application. One fee must be paid per application and per person. In other words, each applicant who is not exempt must pay a fee for each application he/she submits. The complete list can be found on the website of Belgian Immigration Office: https://dofi.ibz.be/en/themes/faq/fee (https://dofi.ibz.be/en/themes/faq/fee). Please visit to check the details of Exemptions, Amount of the fee, Payment of the fee, Proof of payment of the fee and Refund of the fee. The proof of payment has to be provided together with the visa application.

## Documents Required
To apply for a short term Schengen visa you will need multiple documents. Below are the requirements for getting married in Belgium
Checklist for Marriage
Approval Hospitals and Authorized Doctors (https://china.diplomatie.belgium.be/en/travel-belgium/visa-belgium)
Medical certificate (EN)
Medical certificate (NL)
Medical certificate (French)
Specific insurance requirements

## Photo Specifications
Please click here to get the new photograph requirements.

## Processing Time
Applications received at the Belgian Embassy/Consulate General will be processed within 2 and 15 working days, provided that a complete file is submitted. We recommend that you submit your applications 15 days prior to the intended departure date.
Applications can be submitted up to 180 days in advance.
In case the file has to be submitted for decision to the Immigration Office in Brussels, the processing time may take more than 15 days.
Passports are kept at the Embassy for the duration of the processing of the visa application. Should you have an urgent need for your passport, please contact the Visa Application Centre where your application has been submitted or the Belgian Embassy/Consulate General via email to request a temporary withdrawal of your passport.
For more information on the processing time, please visit the website of the Belgian Ministry of Foreign Affairs (https://dofi.ibz.be/en).
